2026-05-06T19:23:53.2035853Z Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 03:03:22 +0200
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2036158Z Subject: [PATCH 3/3] qsort: fix shift UB in shl and shr
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2036387Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2036551Z if shl() or shr() are called with n==8*sizeof(size_t), n is adjusted
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2036969Z to 0. the shift by (sizeof(size_t) * 8 - n) that then follows will
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2037402Z consequently shift by the width of size_t, which is UB and in practice
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2037772Z produces an incorrect result.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2037938Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2038361Z return early in this case. the bitvector p was already shifted by the
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2038730Z required amount.

2026-05-06T19:23:53.2044638Z >From 67219f0130ec7c876ac0b299046460fad31caabf Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2045028Z From: Rich Felker <dalias@aerifal.cx>
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2045302Z Date: Mon, 30 Mar 2026 16:00:50 -0400
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2045664Z Subject: [PATCH] fix pathological slowness & incorrect mappings in iconv
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2046021Z  gb18030 decoder
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2046138Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2046296Z in order to implement the "UTF" aspect of gb18030 (ability to
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2046715Z represent arbitrary unicode characters not present in the 2-byte
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2047153Z mapping), we have to apply the index obtained from the encoded 4-byte
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2047588Z sequence into the set of unmapped characters. this was done by
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2048409Z scanning repeatedly over the table of mapped characters and counting
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2048931Z off mapped characters below a running index by which to adjust the
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2049386Z running index by on each iteration. this iterative process eventually
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2049833Z leaves us with the value of the Nth unmapped character replacing the
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2050279Z index, but depending on which particular character that is, the number
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2050744Z of iterations needed to find it can be in the tens of thousands, and
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2051178Z each iteration traverses the whole 126x190 table in the inner loop.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2051610Z this can lead to run times exceeding an entire second per character on
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2051967Z moderate-speed machines.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2052112Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2052410Z on top of that, the transformation logic produced wrong results for
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2052831Z BMP characters above the the surrogate range, as a result of not
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2053272Z correctly accounting for it being excluded, and for characters outside
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2053703Z the BMP, as a result of a misunderstanding of how gb18030 encodes
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2054018Z them.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2054113Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2054305Z this patch replaces the unmapped character lookup with a single linear
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2054739Z search of a list of unmapped ranges. there are only 206 such ranges,
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2055165Z and these are permanently assigned and unchangeable as a consequence
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2055601Z of the character encoding having to be stable, so a simple array of
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2056031Z 16-bit start/length values for each range consumes only 824 bytes, a
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2056398Z very reasonable size cost here.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2056565Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2056750Z this new table accounts for the previously-incorrect surrogate
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2057183Z handling, and non-BMP characters are handled correctly by a single
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2057595Z offset, without the need for any unmapped-range search.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2057822Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2057997Z there are still a small number of mappings that are incorrect due to
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2058780Z late changes made in the definition of gb18030, swapping PUA
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2059223Z codepoints with proper Unicode characters. correcting these requires a
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2059629Z postprocessing step that will be added later.

2026-05-06T19:23:53.2114891Z # This script runs `musl-cross-make` to prepare C toolchain (Binutils, GCC, musl itself)
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2115427Z # and builds static libunwind that we distribute for static target.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2115766Z #
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2116102Z # Versions of the toolchain components are configurable in `musl-cross-make/Makefile` and
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2116828Z # musl unlike GLIBC is forward compatible so upgrading it shouldn't break old distributions.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2117355Z # Right now we have: Binutils 2.31.1, GCC 9.2.0, musl 1.2.5.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2117602Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2117697Z # ignore-tidy-linelength
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2117850Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2117922Z set -ex
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2118026Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2118307Z hide_output() {
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2118524Z   set +x
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2118698Z   on_err="
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2118945Z echo ERROR: An error was encountered with the build.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2119247Z cat /tmp/build.log
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2119435Z exit 1
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2119592Z "
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2119760Z   trap "$on_err" ERR
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2120071Z   bash -c "while true; do sleep 30; echo \$(date) - building ...; done" &
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2120569Z   PING_LOOP_PID=$!
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2120770Z   "$@" &> /tmp/build.log
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2120982Z   trap - ERR
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121173Z   kill $PING_LOOP_PID
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121376Z   rm /tmp/build.log
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121564Z   set -x
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121729Z }
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121817Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2121892Z ARCH=$1
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2122072Z TARGET=$ARCH-linux-musl
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2122210Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2122407Z # Don't depend on the mirrors of sabotage linux that musl-cross-make uses.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2122943Z LINUX_HEADERS_SITE=https://ci-mirrors.rust-lang.org/rustc/sabotage-linux-tarballs
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2123373Z LINUX_VER=headers-4.19.88
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2123517Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2123600Z OUTPUT=/usr/local
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2123779Z shift
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2123878Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2124127Z # Ancient binutils versions don't understand debug symbols produced by more recent tools.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2124676Z # Apparently applying `-fPIC` everywhere allows them to link successfully.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2125147Z # Enable debug info. If we don't do so, users can't debug into musl code,
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2125549Z # debuggers can't walk the stack, etc. Fixes #90103.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2125863Z export CFLAGS="-fPIC -g1 $CFLAGS"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2126035Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2126226Z git clone https://github.com/richfelker/musl-cross-make # -b v0.9.9
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2126578Z cd musl-cross-make
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2126849Z # A version that includes support for building musl 1.2.5
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2127201Z git checkout 3635262e4524c991552789af6f36211a335a77b3
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2127417Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2127637Z # Patch CVE-2026-6042: https://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2026/04/09/19
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2128408Z # Patch CVE-2026-40200: https://www.openwall.com/lists/musl/2026/04/10/3
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2128990Z # These should be removed when musl-cross-make adds them, or we upgrade to musl >= 1.2.7.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2129555Z cp /build/musl-cve-2026-6042.diff ./patches/musl-1.2.5/0003-cve-2026-6042.diff
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2130066Z cp /build/musl-cve-2026-40200.diff ./patches/musl-1.2.5/0004-cve-2026-40200.diff
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2130371Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2130706Z hide_output make -j$(nproc) TARGET=$TARGET MUSL_VER=1.2.5 LINUX_HEADERS_SITE=$LINUX_HEADERS_SITE LINUX_VER=$LINUX_VER
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2131513Z hide_output make install TARGET=$TARGET MUSL_VER=1.2.5 LINUX_HEADERS_SITE=$LINUX_HEADERS_SITE LINUX_VER=$LINUX_VER OUTPUT=$OUTPUT
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2131980Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2132047Z cd -
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2132151Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2132278Z # Install musl library to make binaries executable
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2132631Z ln -s $OUTPUT/$TARGET/lib/libc.so /lib/ld-musl-$ARCH.so.1
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2132980Z echo $OUTPUT/$TARGET/lib >> /etc/ld-musl-$ARCH.path
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2133198Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2133474Z # Now when musl bootstraps itself create proper toolchain symlinks to make build and tests easier
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2133935Z if [ "$REPLACE_CC" = "1" ]; then
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2134180Z     for exec in cc gcc; do
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2134561Z         ln -s $TARGET-gcc /usr/local/bin/$exec
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2134832Z     done
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135020Z     for exec in cpp c++ g++; do
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135277Z         ln -s $TARGET-g++ /usr/local/bin/$exec
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135526Z     done
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135689Z fi
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135850Z #!/bin/sh
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2135948Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136025Z set -ex
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136123Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136216Z NODEJS_VERSION=v20.12.2
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136435Z YARN_VERSION=1.22.22
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136646Z INSTALL_PATH=${1:-/node}
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2136785Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2137039Z url="https://nodejs.org/dist/${NODEJS_VERSION}/node-${NODEJS_VERSION}-linux-x64.tar.xz"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2137463Z curl -sL "$url" | tar -xJ
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2137854Z mv node-${NODEJS_VERSION}-linux-x64 "${INSTALL_PATH}"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2138070Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2138401Z # now, install yarn.
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2138923Z # we call npm through the node binary, because otherwise npm will expect node to be in the PATH
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2139535Z "${INSTALL_PATH}/bin/node" "${INSTALL_PATH}/bin/npm" install --global yarn@${YARN_VERSION}
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2139942Z #!/bin/sh
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140043Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140138Z # ignore-tidy-linelength
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140281Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140357Z set -ex
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140453Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140540Z case "$(uname -m)" in
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2140736Z     x86_64)
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2141153Z         url="https://ci-mirrors.rust-lang.org/rustc/2025-02-24-sccache-v0.10.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2141624Z         ;;
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2141793Z     aarch64)
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2142191Z         url="https://ci-mirrors.rust-lang.org/rustc/2025-02-24-sccache-v0.10.0-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2142663Z         ;;
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2142872Z     *)
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2143094Z         echo "unsupported architecture: $(uname -m)"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2143379Z         exit 1
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2143548Z esac
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2143645Z 
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2143758Z curl -fo /usr/local/bin/sccache "${url}"
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2144035Z chmod +x /usr/local/bin/sccache
2026-05-06T19:23:53.2144260Z x86_64
